# Find Answer in Codebase - Context Window PR

**Repository:** llama.cpp (TheAgentCompany GitLab)
**Difficulty:** MEDIUM
**Category:** ccb_tac
**Task Type:** Code Navigation & Search

## Description

Search the llama.cpp repository to find which pull request improved the context window
for llama3.1 series models. This is a code search and navigation task.

## Task

Your colleague Chen Xinyi wants to know which pull requests in llama.cpp
(http://the-agent-company.com:8929/root/llama.cpp) improves the context window of
llama3.1 series models.

Find the most recent related merged pull request number (starting with #) and send
the number to Chen Xinyi. You can find her on RocketChat (http://the-agent-company.com:3000/).

## Success Criteria

[x] Correct PR number (#8676) found and communicated to Chen Xinyi via RocketChat

## Notes

- This task explicitly tests code navigation and search capabilities
- Requires RocketChat interaction to complete
I'll help you find the pull request that improved the context window for llama3.1 series models in the llama.cpp repository. Let me start by exploring the repository to search for relevant PRs.

: fix docker build number and tag name","description":"## Motivation

Currently, docker build (both from CI and locally via `docker build`) outputs build 0 when asking with `--version`

```bash
# docker run --rm ghcr.io/ggerganov/llama.cpp:server --version
version: 0 (unknown)
built with cc (Ubuntu 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04) 11.4.0 for x86_64-linux-gnu
```

This is due to `.git` being included inside `.dockerignore`, which make the directory not being copied into docker build context.

The CI also have a problem where tag name becomes `:full-cuda--b1-1e43630`, while ideally it should be something like `:full-cuda-b1234`

## In this PR

- Remove `.git` from `.dockerignore`
- For CI, each build on master branch will now have 2 tags:
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}` --> example: `:full-cuda`
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}-b${BUILD_NUMBER}` --> example: `:full-cuda-b1234`

## Test

For testing purpose, I created a cloned repo: https://github.com/ngxson/llama.cpp-fix-ci/pkgs/container/llama.cpp-fix-ci/versions

For testing locally:

```bash
docker build -f ./.devops/llama-server.Dockerfile .
docker run --rm llamacpptest --version
# version: 3830 (6513c323)
```

This adds initial support for reranking to `libllama`, `llama-embeddings` and `llama-server`. I've tested mainly with the following 2 models:

- https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3
- https://huggingface.co/jinaai/jina-reranker-v1-tiny-en (small, useful for CI tests)

The reranking is implemented as a pooling layer of type `LLAMA_POOLING_TYPE_RANK`. When used, `libllama` will attach a classification head at the end of the graph:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/src/llama.cpp#L10246-L10266

The current implementation likely does not cover all types of rerankers, so updates would be necessary in the future to support other types of classifications on a case-by-case basis.

The computed rank scores for each sequence can be accessed via the `llama_get_embeddings_seq()` call:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/include/llama.h#L873-L878

The rank score is stored as a single float.

The server endpoint is designed mostly after https://jina.ai/reranker/, but it is not fully complete. Again, I think it's better to update it on a case-by-case basis + the API is not ideal (e.g. what is the purpose of `top_n`?, why are the document contents returned in the response?).

I started to add server tests, but it will take me more time to write the python code, so I'll create a separate issue for people to help with that:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/examples/server/tests/features/rerank.feature#L19-L25

TODO:

- ~tests~ (left for follow-up PR)

: fix docker build number and tag name","description":"## Motivation

Currently, docker build (both from CI and locally via `docker build`) outputs build 0 when asking with `--version`

```bash
# docker run --rm ghcr.io/ggerganov/llama.cpp:server --version
version: 0 (unknown)
built with cc (Ubuntu 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04) 11.4.0 for x86_64-linux-gnu
```

This is due to `.git` being included inside `.dockerignore`, which make the directory not being copied into docker build context.

The CI also have a problem where tag name becomes `:full-cuda--b1-1e43630`, while ideally it should be something like `:full-cuda-b1234`

## In this PR

- Remove `.git` from `.dockerignore`
- For CI, each build on master branch will now have 2 tags:
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}` --> example: `:full-cuda`
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}-b${BUILD_NUMBER}` --> example: `:full-cuda-b1234`

## Test

For testing purpose, I created a cloned repo: https://github.com/ngxson/llama.cpp-fix-ci/pkgs/container/llama.cpp-fix-ci/versions

For testing locally:

```bash
docker build -f ./.devops/llama-server.Dockerfile .
docker run --rm llamacpptest --version
# version: 3830 (6513c323)
```

This adds initial support for reranking to `libllama`, `llama-embeddings` and `llama-server`. I've tested mainly with the following 2 models:

- https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3
- https://huggingface.co/jinaai/jina-reranker-v1-tiny-en (small, useful for CI tests)

The reranking is implemented as a pooling layer of type `LLAMA_POOLING_TYPE_RANK`. When used, `libllama` will attach a classification head at the end of the graph:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/src/llama.cpp#L10246-L10266

The current implementation likely does not cover all types of rerankers, so updates would be necessary in the future to support other types of classifications on a case-by-case basis.

The computed rank scores for each sequence can be accessed via the `llama_get_embeddings_seq()` call:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/include/llama.h#L873-L878

The rank score is stored as a single float.

The server endpoint is designed mostly after https://jina.ai/reranker/, but it is not fully complete. Again, I think it's better to update it on a case-by-case basis + the API is not ideal (e.g. what is the purpose of `top_n`?, why are the document contents returned in the response?).

I started to add server tests, but it will take me more time to write the python code, so I'll create a separate issue for people to help with that:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/examples/server/tests/features/rerank.feature#L19-L25

TODO:

- ~tests~ (left for follow-up PR)
- [x] clean-up
- [x] optimize classification head

Model: https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3

Testing:

```bash
python3 convert_hf_to_gguf.py \
	~/Data/huggingface/bge-reranker-v2-m3/ \
    --outfile models/bge-reranker-v2-m3/ggml-model-f16.gguf \
    --outtype f16
```

Classifier:

https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/5af7d41e49bbfc8319f462eb45253dcb3863dfb7/src/transformers/models/roberta/modeling_roberta.py#L1566

: fix docker build number and tag name","description":"## Motivation

Currently, docker build (both from CI and locally via `docker build`) outputs build 0 when asking with `--version`

```bash
# docker run --rm ghcr.io/ggerganov/llama.cpp:server --version
version: 0 (unknown)
built with cc (Ubuntu 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04) 11.4.0 for x86_64-linux-gnu
```

This is due to `.git` being included inside `.dockerignore`, which make the directory not being copied into docker build context.

The CI also have a problem where tag name becomes `:full-cuda--b1-1e43630`, while ideally it should be something like `:full-cuda-b1234`

## In this PR

- Remove `.git` from `.dockerignore`
- For CI, each build on master branch will now have 2 tags:
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}` --> example: `:full-cuda`
  - `:${{ matrix.config.tag }}-b${BUILD_NUMBER}` --> example: `:full-cuda-b1234`

## Test

For testing purpose, I created a cloned repo: https://github.com/ngxson/llama.cpp-fix-ci/pkgs/container/llama.cpp-fix-ci/versions

For testing locally:

```bash
docker build -f ./.devops/llama-server.Dockerfile .
docker run --rm llamacpptest --version
# version: 3830 (6513c323)
```

This adds initial support for reranking to `libllama`, `llama-embeddings` and `llama-server`. I've tested mainly with the following 2 models:

- https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3
- https://huggingface.co/jinaai/jina-reranker-v1-tiny-en (small, useful for CI tests)

The reranking is implemented as a pooling layer of type `LLAMA_POOLING_TYPE_RANK`. When used, `libllama` will attach a classification head at the end of the graph:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/src/llama.cpp#L10246-L10266

The current implementation likely does not cover all types of rerankers, so updates would be necessary in the future to support other types of classifications on a case-by-case basis.

The computed rank scores for each sequence can be accessed via the `llama_get_embeddings_seq()` call:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/include/llama.h#L873-L878

The rank score is stored as a single float.

The server endpoint is designed mostly after https://jina.ai/reranker/, but it is not fully complete. Again, I think it's better to update it on a case-by-case basis + the API is not ideal (e.g. what is the purpose of `top_n`?, why are the document contents returned in the response?).

I started to add server tests, but it will take me more time to write the python code, so I'll create a separate issue for people to help with that:

https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/4d457755c08c4a59a3436959888da81399a25e5d/examples/server/tests/features/rerank.feature#L19-L25

[{"id":6964,"iid":8048,"project_id":7,"title":"gguf-hash: model wide and per tensor hashing using xxhash and sha1","description":"*Created by: mofosyne*

This is a WIP PR proposal for layer hashing and model hashing of each layer of a gguf model

I previously did an experiment attempting to make the hashing process independent of quantisation, but that turns out to have too many technical issue and plus the use case for such feature is uncertain.

This PR on the other hands focus only on just hashing each tensors as an opaque data area without caring to decode the content.

The application for this feature is as part of some ci flow so instead of storing test files outputs you can just store the expected hash output. You can use this then to check for regression. For this reason i added xxhash as it is a much faster than sha1 in hashing, but left sha1 in because it is more widely supported (e.g. built into python)

I also for the python hash implementation added a UUIDv5 generator, I plan to add that to the c side if it makes sense.
As one of my idea is that every model would have a unique UUID based on the model content. Would be happy to hear feedback about this as I plan to include it during model conversion processes. e.g.

```
UUIDv5  15608c46-42f1-50ae-b98f-04c394f6806f  phi-2.Q6_K.gguf
```

Note that for the global model wide hashing, I just hash every tensor in the order that was dumped from the gguf file... so if the tensor order is swapped in the file then the hash will likely change.

(For this PR, I decided that KV store hash is outside of scope)

[{"id":6964,"iid":8048,"project_id":7,"title":"gguf-hash: model wide and per tensor hashing using xxhash and sha1","description":"*Created by: mofosyne*

This is a WIP PR proposal for layer hashing and model hashing of each layer of a gguf model

I previously did an experiment attempting to make the hashing process independent of quantisation, but that turns out to have too many technical issue and plus the use case for such feature is uncertain.

This PR on the other hands focus only on just hashing each tensors as an opaque data area without caring to decode the content.

The application for this feature is as part of some ci flow so instead of storing test files outputs you can just store the expected hash output. You can use this then to check for regression. For this reason i added xxhash as it is a much faster than sha1 in hashing, but left sha1 in because it is more widely supported (e.g. built into python)

I also for the python hash implementation added a UUIDv5 generator, I plan to add that to the c side if it makes sense.
As one of my idea is that every model would have a unique UUID based on the model content. Would be happy to hear feedback about this as I plan to include it during model conversion processes. e.g.

```
UUIDv5  15608c46-42f1-50ae-b98f-04c394f6806f  phi-2.Q6_K.gguf
```

Note that for the global model wide hashing, I just hash every tensor in the order that was dumped from the gguf file... so if the tensor order is swapped in the file then the hash will likely change.

(For this PR, I decided that KV store hash is outside of scope)

[{"id":6964,"iid":8048,"project_id":7,"title":"gguf-hash: model wide and per tensor hashing using xxhash and sha1","description":"*Created by: mofosyne*

This is a WIP PR proposal for layer hashing and model hashing of each layer of a gguf model

I previously did an experiment attempting to make the hashing process independent of quantisation, but that turns out to have too many technical issue and plus the use case for such feature is uncertain.

This PR on the other hands focus only on just hashing each tensors as an opaque data area without caring to decode the content.

The application for this feature is as part of some ci flow so instead of storing test files outputs you can just store the expected hash output. You can use this then to check for regression. For this reason i added xxhash as it is a much faster than sha1 in hashing, but left sha1 in because it is more widely supported (e.g. built into python)

I also for the python hash implementation added a UUIDv5 generator, I plan to add that to the c side if it makes sense.
As one of my idea is that every model would have a unique UUID based on the model content. Would be happy to hear feedback about this as I plan to include it during model conversion processes. e.g.

```
UUIDv5  15608c46-42f1-50ae-b98f-04c394f6806f  phi-2.Q6_K.gguf
```

Note that for the global model wide hashing, I just hash every tensor in the order that was dumped from the gguf file... so if the tensor order is swapped in the file then the hash will likely change.

(For this PR, I decided that KV store hash is outside of scope)

[{"id":6964,"iid":8048,"project_id":7,"title":"gguf-hash: model wide and per tensor hashing using xxhash and sha1","description":"*Created by: mofosyne*

This is a WIP PR proposal for layer hashing and model hashing of each layer of a gguf model

I previously did an experiment attempting to make the hashing process independent of quantisation, but that turns out to have too many technical issue and plus the use case for such feature is uncertain.

This PR on the other hands focus only on just hashing each tensors as an opaque data area without caring to decode the content.

The application for this feature is as part of some ci flow so instead of storing test files outputs you can just store the expected hash output. You can use this then to check for regression. For this reason i added xxhash as it is a much faster than sha1 in hashing, but left sha1 in because it is more widely supported (e.g. built into python)

I also for the python hash implementation added a UUIDv5 generator, I plan to add that to the c side if it makes sense.
As one of my idea is that every model would have a unique UUID based on the model content. Would be happy to hear feedback about this as I plan to include it during model conversion processes. e.g.

```
UUIDv5  15608c46-42f1-50ae-b98f-04c394f6806f  phi-2.Q6_K.gguf
```

Note that for the global model wide hashing, I just hash every tensor in the order that was dumped from the gguf file... so if the tensor order is swapped in the file then the hash will likely change.

(For this PR, I decided that KV store hash is outside of scope)

steps.message:
I've successfully found the pull request! Here are my findings:

**PR Number: #8676**
**Title:** "Add llama 3.1 rope scaling factors to llama conversion and inference"

**Description excerpt:** "This commit generates the rope factors on conversion and adds them to the resulting model as a tensor. At inference time, these factors are passed to the `ggml_rope_ext` rope operation. From our testing, this really improves results for context windows above 8192 for Llama 3.1."

This PR directly addresses context window improvements for Llama 3.1 series models by implementing rope scaling factors that enable better performance with context windows larger than 8192 tokens.

**Regarding RocketChat Communication:**
Unfortunately, I'm unable to access the RocketChat service (port 3000) from this environment as the service connection is refused. However, you can use the following information to message Chen Xinyi:

**Message to send to Chen Xinyi:**
"Hi Chen Xinyi, regarding the context window improvement for llama3.1 series models in llama.cpp - the most recent related merged PR is **#8676** titled 'Add llama 3.1 rope scaling factors to llama conversion and inference'. This PR implements rope scaling factors that significantly improve results for context windows above 8192 tokens."

You would need to access RocketChat directly at http://the-agent-company.com:3000/ to send this message to her.
